name: Network Ping Sweep
description: Discovers live hosts on a network without port scanning. Uses ICMP echo, TCP SYN to 443, TCP ACK to 80, and ICMP timestamp.
expected_output:
Nmap scan report for 192.168.1.1
Host is up (0.0010s latency).
Nmap scan report for 192.168.1.10
Host is up (0.0025s latency).
5 hosts up
use_case: First step in network reconnaissance — identify which hosts are alive before detailed scanning.
prerequisites: Root for ICMP (or use -PS/-PA for unprivileged)
detections: Multiple ARP requests from single host
common_mistakes: Hosts may be up but not respond to ping (use -Pn for those)

name: Aggressive Scan (All-in-One)
description: Combines OS detection, version detection, script scanning, and traceroute in a single command. Fast and comprehensive.
expected_output:
PORT   STATE SERVICE VERSION
22/tcp open  ssh     OpenSSH 8.9
|_ssh-hostkey: ...
80/tcp open  http    Apache 2.4.52
|_http-title: Welcome
OS: Linux 5.x
Traceroute: 2 hops
use_case: Quick comprehensive scan when stealth isn't a concern — gets maximum info in one command.
detections: Multiple scan types visible in logs

name: RustScan — Ultra-Fast Port Discovery
description: Scans all 65535 ports in seconds, then passes open ports to nmap for service detection. Best of both worlds.

arguments.-- -sV -sC: Pass these flags to nmap for open ports

expected_output:
Open 192.168.1.10:22
Open 192.168.1.10:80
Open 192.168.1.10:443
[~] Starting Nmap on open ports...
use_case: Fastest way to find all open ports then get detailed service info — replaces masscan + nmap workflow.
detections: Extremely high connection rate triggers IDS
common_mistakes: Setting ulimit too high (crashes target or network)

name: DNS Reconnaissance
description: Comprehensive DNS enumeration — standard records, brute-force subdomains, and attempt zone transfers.

arguments.-t std: Standard record enumeration (A, AAAA, MX, NS, SOA, TXT)

expected_output:
[*] SOA ns1.example.com 93.184.216.34
[*] NS ns1.example.com 93.184.216.34
[*] MX mail.example.com 93.184.216.35
[*] A www.example.com 93.184.216.34
use_case: Map all DNS records for a domain — reveals mail servers, name servers, and potentially internal hostnames.
detections: Zone transfer attempts in DNS logs

name: Wayback Machine URL Discovery
description: Fetches all URLs ever archived by the Wayback Machine for a domain. Reveals old endpoints, parameters, and forgotten files.
use_case: Find forgotten endpoints, old admin panels, backup files, and parameters that may still be accessible.
detections: Passive — queries Wayback Machine, not the target
remediation: Request removal from Wayback Machine if needed
common_mistakes: Not checking if URLs are still live (use httpx to verify)
